title: Gothic art (quiz)
topic_path: Test your knowledge of Gothic art

assessment_items.question_content:
**Gothic architecture used mostly rounded arches.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Gothic architecture used pointed arches, which were more versatile, and ultimately, combined with groin vaults, allowed architects to create higher ceilings and bigger windows.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f7e2040ae46afec72fdb1a29d585789.jpeg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The north portal of Chartres Cathedral starts with the Creation story in the outer archivolts and ends with this scene in the tympanum . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The tympanum holds a scene of the Virgin enthroned at her coronation.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/00c59a87c2186d039eb9d28489597ae4.jpeg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The three parts of the Gothic nave elevation are . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The three parts of the Gothic nave elevation are the arcade below, the triforium, and the clerestory above.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4c1ca52678ea7712653581296d41eedd.jpeg)

choices.content: the ambulatory, the gallery, and the groin vaults.
the archivolts, the triforium, and the flying buttresses.
the arcade, the triforium, and the clerestory.
the choir, the gallery, and the clerestory.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The façade of the Cathedral of Notre Dame de Chartres was organized according to the Golden Ratio.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Late medieval architects were interested in creating visual harmony according to ancient principles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Berlinghieri's *St. Francis Altarpiece* emphasizes the ways in which St. Francis is like Christ.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The altarpiece emphasizes the stigmata and healing miracles of St. Francis, which show him to be like Christ.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8533e00fa2c2778390f38d65127fbc95.jpeg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is not true about the Cathedral of Notre Dame de Chartres?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The architect used pointed arches, thin walls, slender piers and flying buttresses to support the weight of the building externally at least in part. This allowed for expansive windows such as the one below.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/14f90f6ddbf328771ff40dff98f90ab7.jpeg)

choices.content: The Gothic features of the cathedral emphasized the idea that the building was a divine and heavenly space.
The architect used rounded arches, thick walls, and massive piers to support the weight of the building internally.
This Gothic church is full of symbols. An important symbol in this church is the light, which was seen as a symbol of divine presence.
Gothic architects tried to maximize the space for stained glass windows. The development of rib vaults and flying buttresses made this easier.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Abbot Suger wanted to create an architectural style that would express the growing power of . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/146a8181d5f2700b7f6340259863f287.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Abbot Suger wanted to create an architectural style that would express the growing power of the French monarchy.
